item.title: 使用 Python 和 PyQt5 动态编写 | Write Dynamically with Python & PyQt5
item.description:
杰森克鲁兹

发表于假设您已经知道如何使用可以使用 pip 轻松安装的 QT Designer 程序。 创建一个动态文件不应该那么难，对我来说确实如此。 我上下搜索了 2.5 天，直到我随机找到一个有趣的站点，该站点有一个逐步设置的方法，可以尝试使用您在 Qt Designer 中保存设计时获得的 interface.ui 文件创建动态 python 文件。

使用 Qt Designer 时，保存您的工作后，它将保存在 UI 和 QRC 文件中。 我以前的做事方式是，一旦我将 interface.ui 文件转换为 interface.py 文件，我就忘记了我的图标，因为使用 Qt Designer 和 python，这些不会为你导入。 我在这个问题上挣扎了好几天。

真快。

您还必须将资源文件 (resources.qrc) 导入 python 文件。 使用此命令：

pyrcc5 resources.qrc -o resources.py

一旦你创建了你的 python 文件，你会想要将它导入到你正在创建的动态文件中。 如下例：

主文件

或者无论你想调用什么文件，这个文件将运行你的新 python 文件，并且从这里你可以对程序的机制进行所有更正，而 UI 是不同文件中的骨架模式。 当您必须对 UI 文件进行更改时，这很有帮助，这样您就不会一遍又一遍地覆盖您编写的代码。

希望有帮助！

欢迎他们加入 DEV 并分享一些关于你自己的信息。

一旦暂停，jasoncruzdev 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，jasoncruzdev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，jasoncruzdev 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jasoncruzdev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 jasoncruzdev 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 jasoncruzdev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jason Cruz

Posted on Sep 10

Assuming you already know how to use the QT Designer program that can be easily installed using pip. Creating a dynamic file shouldnt be that hard to make, for me it was. I searched high and low for 2.5 days until I randomly found an interesting site that had a set by step way to try creating a dynamic python file using your interface.ui file you get when you save your design inside Qt Designer.

2 Important Things

When working with Qt Designer, after saving your work, it will be saved inside a UI & QRC file. My old way of doing things was once I converted my interface.ui file to interface.py file, I forgot my icons, because with Qt Designer and python, those are not imported for you. I struggled for days on this issue.

Real quick.

You must import your resource file (resources.qrc) also to a python file. Using this command:

pyrcc5 resources.qrc -o resources.py

Once you have created your python file, you wil want to import it into your dynamic file you are creating. As example below:

Main.py

Or whatever you would like to call that file, this file will run your new python file, and from here you can make all the corrections to the mechanics of your program while the UI is the skeleton mode in a different file. This is helpful when you have to make changes to your UI file, this way you wont be overwriting your code that you wrote over and over again.

Hope that helps!

Welcome them to DEV and share a bit about yourself.

item.title: 使用 RFC-6598 (100.64.0.0/10) 扩展 RFC1918 | Extending RFC1918 with RFC-6598 (100.64.0.0/10)
item.description:
阿米尔·侯赛尼

发表于我们都熟悉 RFC-1918 AKA 私有 IP 空间（10.xxx、172.16.xx、192.168.xx），称为私有 IP 地址，因为只要复制的 IP 分配的设备不满足，任何用户都可以复制它们在同一个 L3 网络中。

但是由于公共 IPv4 空间的耗尽，IANA 已经分配了第二个地址空间，例如 RFC-1918，这一次虽然RFC-6598和它自 2012 年以来被称为共享地址空间，但它最初打算用于 CGNAT（ Carrier Grade Natting），IANA 在标准中声明，它也可以以与 RFC-1918 地址相同的方式使用，引自 RFC-6598：

共享 CGN 空间的使用 共享地址空间是指定给服务提供商使用的 IPv4 地址空间，目的是促进 CGN 部署。 此外，共享地址空间可用作路由设备上的附加非全局可路由空间，当两个不同接口上的地址相同时，该设备能够跨路由器接口进行地址转换。

新分配的地址空间是100.64.0.0/10 ，你可能很熟悉，因为我是第一次在数据中心的设备环回接口中使用这个地址空间，无论是网络设备还是裸机服务器，这就是为什么我继续去研究这个 IP Space 的使用情况。

在我们继续之前，先让您了解一下 CGNAT 是什么。

在下图中非常简单地描述了：

之前：每个家庭 CPE 都被分配了一个公共 IP 地址，用于直接访问互联网。
之后：家庭 CPE 被分配 RFC-6589 IP 地址 (100.64.0.0/10)，该地址将在您的 ISP 边缘被 Natted 到公共 IP 范围，仍然是一对一的 NAT，但您只需要一个公共 IP 地址如果您想访问外部资源，只要您留在运营商的网络中，您就会使用您的 RFC-6598 IP。

您可能会问为什么不将 RFC-1918 IP 用于家庭 CPE，虽然它可能会导致运营商出现操作问题。

运营商应该开始做的考虑之一是将 100.64.0.0/10 包含在来自其他运营商的传入路由过滤器列表中作为安全中介，就像所有运营商对 RFC-1918 范围所做的那样，引用 RFC。

有关共享地址空间网络的路由信息​​不得跨服务提供者边界传播。 服务提供商必须过滤有关共享地址空间的传入广告。

回到我们的主题

特殊用途 IP 地址

由于我们正在查看其中一个新地址空间，因此让我们借此机会了解 IANA 在RFC-5735中规定的一些不熟悉的特殊用途 IP 范围

这是为了扩展您对 RFC 规范的了解，但并非所有网络工程师都遵守那些测试 IP 范围，我们大多只在操作空间中看到合规性。

地址空间用法
这是“链接本地”块。 如 [RFC3927] 中所述，它被分配用于单个链路上的主机之间的通信。 主机通过自动配置获取这些地址，例如在找不到 DHCP 服务器时。
该块被指定为“TEST-NET”，用于文档和示例代码。 在供应商和协议文档中，它通常与域名 example.com 或 example.net 结合使用
该块被分配用作 6to4 中继任播地址
此块已分配用于网络互连设备的基准测试，分配此范围是为了尽量减少在测试设备意外连接到 Internet 的一部分时发生冲突的机会。

问答

  • 为什么不应该在企业网络中使用共享地址空间范围？ 何时应在企业中使用共享地址空间 IP？ 如果您用完了 IPv4 私有地址并且无法迁移到 IPv6，请不要使用它们与您的服务提供商互连，也不要向他们宣传您的服务提供商。
  • 它在哪里使用？ 一些数据中心提供商和云在底层中使用这些地址，因为它们不太可能与可能在 RFC-1918 范围内的任何覆盖客户的地址发生冲突。
  • 为什么云提供商转向在其数据中心使用共享地址空间？ 一开始，我认为当我们在数据中心底层使用它时，我们将是唯一这样做的人，但这似乎是云提供商的行业广泛方向，发现 VMWare 也这样做，您可以查看VMware SDDC
  • 私有/共享地址范围中有多少个可用地址？ 地址范围 | 以下是有用的 RFC，可用于了解推送背后的原因以及实施 CGNAT 的运营商面临的问题

贡献代码库或托管您自己的代码库。

看看这些！ 主要 Forem 回购

  • 自托管说明

    • 一旦暂停，amrelhusseiny 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，amrelhusseiny 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发表，amrelhusseiny 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 amrelhusseiny 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 amrelhusseiny 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 amrelhusseiny 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Amr ElHusseiny

    Posted on Sep 10

    We are all familiar with the RFC-1918 AKA Private IP Spaces (10.x.x.x , 172.16.x.x , 192.168.x.x), called private IP addresses because they can be duplicated by any user as long as the duplicated IP assigned devices does not meet in same L3 network.

    But due to the exhaustion of the Public IPv4 space , IANA has assigned a second address space like RFC-1918 , this time though RFC-6598 and its called the Shared Address space since 2012 , while it was originaly intended to be used for CGNAT (Carrier Grade Natting), IANA stated in the standard, that it also can be used in the same manner as RFC-1918 addresses , quoting from RFC-6598:

    Use of Shared CGN Space Shared Address Space is IPv4 address space designated for Service Provider use with the purpose of facilitating CGN deployment. Also, Shared Address Space can be used as additional non-globally routable space on routing equipment that is able to do address translation across router interfaces when the addresses are identical on two different interfaces.

    The newly assigned address space is the 100.64.0.0/10, you may be familiar with, for me was 1st exposed to the usage of this address space in Datacenters's devices loopback interfaces, wherther network devices or baremetal servers, thats why I went on a journy to do research on the usage of this IP Space .

    to pivot a little bit for you to understand what is CGNAT before we continue.

    CGNAT (Carrier Grade NAT)

    Described very simply in the following diagram :

    Before : each home CPE was assigned a Public IP address to use to reach internet directly .
    After : Home CPEs are assigned RFC-6589 IP Address (100.64.0.0/10) that will be Natted to Public IP ranges on the edge of your ISP , still the NATing is one to one , but you will only need a Public IP address if you want to reach external resources , as long as you stay in your carrier's network you will beusing your RFC-6598 IP.

    You may ask why not just use RFC-1918 IPs for home CPE, while its possible, it showed to cause operational issue with the carriers.

    One of the considerations that carriers should start doing , is including the 100.64.0.0/10 in their incoming route filter list from other carriers as a security mediation , just like all carriers do with the RFC-1918 ranges , quoting the RFC.

    routing information about Shared Address Space networks MUST NOT be propagated across Service Provider boundaries. Service Providers MUST filter incoming advertisements regarding Shared Address Space.

    back to our main topic

    Special Use IP Addresses

    Since we are having a look at one of the new address spaces, lets take this opportunity to get to know some of the unfamiliar special use IP ranges stated by IANA in RFC-5735

    This is to extend your knowledge of the RFCs specs , but not all Networks Engineers adhere to those testing IP ranges, we mostly see the compliance in the Operational spaces only .

    Address Space Usage
    169.254.0.0/16 This is the "link local" block. As described in [RFC3927], it is allocated for communication between hosts on a single link. Hosts obtain these addresses by auto-configuration, such as when a DHCP server cannot be found.
    192.0.2.0/24 , 198.51.100.0/24, This block is assigned as "TEST-NET" for use in documentation and example code. It is often used in conjunction with domain names example.com or example.net in vendor and protocol documentation
    192.88.99.0/24 This block is allocated for use as 6to4 relay anycast addresses
    198.18.0.0/15 This block has been allocated for use in benchmark tests of network interconnect devices, this range was assigned to minimize the chance of conflict in case a testing device were to be accidentally connected to part of the Internet.

    Q&A

    • Why shouldn't use Shared Address Space range in your Enterprise network ? , in the renound Ivan Pepelnjak article , he argues that you should never use this IP address space (100.64.0.0/10) as its not considered Private and it will result in issues if you are using that address space in your enterprise sites at the same time that your Servic Provider is using CGNAT as he would probably using IPs from the same space on his CPE external interface .
    • When you should use Shared Address Space IPs in your enterprise ? If you ran out of IPv4 private addresses and you are unable to migrate to IPv6 , DONOT use them to interconnect with your service provider and DONOT advertise them your service provider.
    • Where is it used ? some Datacenter providers and clouds use these addresses in the underlay as they are less likely to conflict with any overlay customer's address which probably is in the RFC-1918 ranges.
    • Why Cloud providers are pivoting to use the Shared Address Space in their Data centers? At 1st I thought when we used it in our Datacenter underlay that we would be the only ones doing that, but it seems to be an industry wide direction in the Cloud providers specifically, found that also VMWare does the same , you can check VMware SDDC
    • How many addresses available in the Private/Shared address ranges ? | Address Range | Number of IPv4 addresses | |----------------|--------------------------| | 10.0.0.0/8 | 16 Million addresses | | 172.16.0.0/12 | 1 Million addresses | | 192.168.0.0/16 | 65536 Addresses | | 100.64.0.0/10 | |

    Following are useful RFCs to understand the reason behind the push and the issues faced by carriers implementing CGNAT

     Contribute to the codebase or host your own. 

    Check these out! 👇

    • Main Forem Repo
    • Self-Host Instructions

    Once unsuspended, amrelhusseiny will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    If amrelhusseiny is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 垃圾收集器（GC）概述 | Overview of Garbage Collector(GC)
item.description:
在本文中，我们将了解垃圾收集器（GC）和一些内存泄漏。

在我们进入垃圾收集器之前，我们将首先学习，

什么是内存泄漏？

内存泄漏是一种未使用且无法从内存 (RAM) 释放或释放的资源泄漏。

内存泄漏何时发生，结果如何？

当 GC 无法释放未使用的对象/资源时会发生内存泄漏。 因此，它将减少可用内存量并影响应用程序的性能。 然而，内存并不总是满的。 这意味着存在内存泄漏。 有时应用程序可能会使用大量内存来执行繁重的任务。

如何防止内存泄漏？

除了垃圾收集器之外，开发人员的工作就是以这样一种方式进行编程，以便在需要的地方释放不需要的资源/对象。 防止内存泄漏的另一个好方法是尽量减少静态成员的使用。

在极少数情况下，您会在应用程序运行时遇到java.lang.OutOfMemoryError 。 这是因为 JVM 无法为该应用程序分配内存（堆空间）。 同时不要忘记垃圾收集器在从堆内存分配/释放对象中起着重要作用。

什么是垃圾收集器？

通常，每当我们打开一个应用程序时，操作系统都会为该应用程序分配一定数量的内存。 它将使用某些 GC 步骤定期运行。

垃圾收集器步骤：

最初，当在堆中创建一个对象时，它将是相应对象的头节点，当相应对象持有其他对象的引用时，它将创建一个类似图形的结构。 拥有该 GC 将遵循三个步骤来执行垃圾收集。

标记： GC 将从节点的开头遍历对象图并标记可达的对象。

压缩：删除不可达对象后剩余的空白空间将通过此步骤进行碎片整理。 此步骤将有助于隔离正在使用和空闲的内存。

垃圾收集器图：

垃圾收集器遍历内存中的对象图，从 GC 根开始到对应的对象。

垃圾收集器的生成：

基本上，堆中的对象分为几代 - Young 和 Old（Tenured）

年轻一代：
最初，对象是在年轻代中创建的。 它进一步分为：

  • 伊甸空间
  • 幸存者空间（从太空到太空）

伊甸园空间是实际创建对象的地方。 当 Eden 空间已满时，GC 启动并执行次要垃圾收集操作。 然后将标记为可达的对象移动到不同的幸存者空间，稍后在小型 GC 操作期间，可达对象将在幸存者之间来回移动。 最后，经过几代之后，它会被转移到老一代。

老一辈：
对象通常只有在达到最大寿命阈值限制时才会从幸存者空间移动到老年代。 在老年代，对象将慢慢变得不可访问，并进行主要的 GC 操作以释放为该应用程序分配的堆内存。 但通常这并不是主要 CG 表演的唯一情况。 当堆已满并需要内存来存储对象时，就会发生主要的 GC 操作。

串行GC：
这是在单线程中运行的基本 GC。 它专为小型应用而设计。 所有的 GC 事件都将在一个线程中连续发生。

并行GC：
此 GC 专为在多处理器或多线程环境中运行的大型应用程序而设计。 多线程用于年轻代中发生的次要垃圾收集。

并行旧 GC：
它与 Parallel GC 相同，只是它为年轻代和老年代使用多个线程。

并发 GC：
在并发收集器中，多个线程用于次要垃圾收集，其算法与并行 GC 中使用的算法相同。 它与应用程序线程同时运行，以避免“停止世界”的情况。 此 GC 使用的 CPU 比任何其他 GC 都多。

垃圾优先GC：
这是专门为具有较大堆大小的多线程应用程序设计的。 此 GC 与其他 GC 完全不同，因为不会有年轻代和老年代。 取而代之的是一组相同大小的区域，并使用多个线程来扫描它们。

厄普西隆气相色谱：
它与 JDK 11 一起发布。它处理内存分配，但不提供内存回收机制。 一旦分配的堆满了，JVM就会关闭。 这种 GC 很少使用，仅适用于对超延迟敏感的应用。

中关村：
它与 JDK 11 一起发布，并在 JDK 12 中即兴发挥。这主要用于需要低延迟和/或使用非常大堆的应用程序。

雪兰多：
它与 JDK 12 一起发布。它比任何其他 GC 更密集，因为它清理垃圾、压缩活动对象并立即将 RAM 释放回操作系统。

简短的摘要：

可访问的对象将保留在内存中，而无法访问的对象将被删除以释放应用程序空间。 我希望这篇文章能帮助你了解垃圾收集器。

学习愉快！！



原文:

In this article we will learn about Garbage Collector(GC) and a little bit of Memory Leak.

Before we jump into the Garbage Collector we will first learn,

What is memory leak?

Memory leak is a type of resource leak that is unused and failed to deallocate or release from memory(RAM).

When does memory leak occur and what is the outcome?

Memory leak occurs when GC could not release unused objects/resources. As a result it will reduce the amount of available memory and affect the performance of the application. However, it is not always the case where memory is full; it means there is a memory leak. Sometimes applications might be using a high amount of memory to perform heavy tasks.

How to prevent memory leak?

Apart from garbage collector, it is the work of the developer to program in such a way that it releases unneeded resources/objects wherever it is required. Another good way to prevent memory leak is by minimizing the use of static members.

On rare occasions you would have faced java.lang.OutOfMemoryError during application runtime. This is because JVM could not able allocate memory(heap space) for that application. Java programmers, please note that calling a static method (System.gc()) manually will not guarantee that it will remove all unreachable objects. At the same time don’t forget garbage collector plays a major role in allocating/deallocating objects from heap memory.

What is Garbage Collector?

GC is an automatic memory manager that manages the allocation and release of memory for an application.

Generally, whenever we open an application, the operating system will allocate a certain amount of memory for that application. GC will manage that memory throughout the application lifetime. It will run periodically with certain GC steps.

Garbage Collector Steps:

Initially, when an object is created in the heap, it will be a head node for the corresponding object and it will create a graph-like structure when the corresponding object holds the reference of others. Having that GC will follow three steps to perform garbage collection.

Mark: GC will walk through the object graph right from the beginning of the node and mark the objects that are reachable.

Sweep/Delete: It will ignore the objects that are marked as reachable and sweep off unreachable objects.

Compacting: The empty space left over after removing the unreachable objects will be defragmented by this step. This step will help to isolate memory that is being used and free.

Garbage Collector Graph:

The garbage collector traverses through the object graph in memory, starting from GC root to corresponding objects.

Generation of Garbage Collector:

Basically, objects in the heap are divided into generations - Young and Old(Tenured)

Young Generation:
Initially, objects are created in the young generation. It is further divided into:

  • Eden space
  • Survivor space (from space and to space)

Eden space is where objects are actually created. When Eden space gets full, GC kicks in and performs a minor garbage collection operation. The objects which are marked as reachable are then moved to different Survivor spaces and later during minor GC operation, reachable objects will be moving to and fro between survivors. Finally, after several generations it will be moved to the old generation.

Old Generation:
Objects are usually moved from survivor space to the old generation only when the maximum tenuring threshold limit has reached. In the old generation, objects will slowly become unreachable and subjected to major GC operations to free up heap memory allocated for that application. But often that is not the only case where major CG performed. When the heap is full and wants memory to store an object then a major GC operation occurs.

Types of GC:

GC in Java are subdivided into:

Serial GC:
This is the basic GC that runs in a single thread. It is specially designed for smaller applications. All the GC events will happen serially with a single thread.

Parallel GC:
This GC is specially designed for large applications that run on multiprocessor or multithreaded environments. Multiple threads are used for minor garbage collection that occurs in young generation.

Parallel Old GC:
It is the same as Parallel GC except that it uses multiple threads for both young and old generations.

Concurrent GC:
In a concurrent collector, multiple threads are used in minor garbage collection with the same algorithm used in parallel GC. It runs concurrently along with application threads to avoid “stop the world” situation. This GC uses more CPU than any other GCs.

Garbage First GC:
This is specially designed for multithreaded applications that have larger heap size. This GC is quite different from other GCs as there won’t be Young and Old generation. Instead there will be a set of equal size regions and uses multiple threads to scan them.

Epsilon GC:
This was released along with JDK 11. It handles memory allocation but doesn’t provide a memory reclamation mechanism. Once the allocated heap is full, JVM will shutdown. This GC is very rarely used and fit only for ultra-latency-sensitive applications.

ZGC:
This was released along with JDK 11 and improvised in JDK 12. This is mainly intended for applications which require low latency and/or use a very large heap.

Shenandoah:
This was released along with JDK 12. It is more intensive than any other GCs as it cleans garbage, compact live objects, and free up RAM immediately back to OS.

Short Summary:

GC is an automatic memory manager that performs certain operations to allocate and deallocate objects from heap memory. Objects that are reachable will be kept in the memory and objects that are unreachable will be removed to free up space for the application. GC will perform this action until the application lifetime so that it runs smoothly.

I hope this article helped you to understand something about Garbage Collector.

Happy learning!!
item.author: the_name_is_vijay

item.title: RISC-V 上的 OSDev 入门 | Getting started with OSDev on RISC-V
item.description:
通过在M-Labs的合作实习，我在 2020 年下半年初次接触RISC-V后，最近重新对它产生了兴趣。 如果您还没有听说过 RISC-V，它是一个公开记录的 RISC ISA，任何个人或商业硬件供应商都可以自由实施。 除了开放性之外，它还拥有高度模块化的结构，可将规范组织成最小的基本 ISA 以及无数可选扩展，从而实现适用于各种用例的高度灵活的 RISC-V 兼容处理器设计。

为了从程序员的角度熟悉 RISC-V，我在过去两个月（2022 年 8 月至 2022 年 9 月）中尝试了以下内容：

作为探索 RISC-V 领域合乎逻辑的下一步，我决定研究如何通过从头开始编写 RISC-V 操作系统来完全控制硬件。

当前进展 (2022-09-10)

在过去的几天里，我设法用 C 语言实现了一个基本的 RISC-V 操作系统内核，它除了将东西打印到串行控制台之外几乎没有。 尽管如此，我还是尽我所能来构建项目以促进中长期的发展。 我将在此基础上构建的项目模板在 GitHub 上发布为maRVelOS 的 v0.0.1 代号为“Meaty Skeleton” ，所以如果您也对 RISC-V 和作为程序员的开放硬件感兴趣，请随时跟着！

我还注意到，在撰写本文时， OSDev Wiki缺少有关RISC -V 的文章和资源，因此我借此机会在那里创建了一个帐户，并在希望它对更广泛的 OSDev 社区有用。 正如您可能已经猜到的那样，OSDev 代表“操作系统开发”，而 OSDev 社区是操作系统开发爱好者聚集在一起交流知识和想法的地方。

资源和致谢

我要感谢RISC-V 从头开始​​和OS 历险记的作者，他们的高质量文章详细介绍了 RISC-V 的工作原理。 如果没有他们写得很好的文章，我不会知道如何开始在 RISC-V 上进行内核开发。 我最初的项目设置 - 汇编中的最小 C 运行时crt0.s和链接器脚本riscv64-virt.lds - 基于前者，而我的 UART 驱动程序代码改编自后者，我打算密切关注后者.

对于交叉编译器工具链，我参考了 OSDev wiki 上的相关页面，并改编了 OSDev 和（Beyond）Linux From Scratch的说明，以构建面向 64 位 RISC-V 的跨 GDB。

我的冒险才刚刚开始

在撰写本文时，maRVelOS 只能将内容打印到控制台。 它仍然无法完成典型操作系统所期望的许多基本功能：

  • 内存管理
  • 流程管理
  • 处理中断
  • 读取和写入文件
  • 在我设法接近可用的临界系统之前，我可能需要很长时间，但希望我能坚持下去，并在此过程中从程序员的角度了解更多有关硬件的信息。 毕竟，OSDev 的主要收获是学习经验——对于开箱即用的生产级通用 OS 内核， Linux就是答案；-)



原文:

I recently re-gained interest in RISC-V after my initial encounter with it in the second half of 2020 through my co-op internship at M-Labs. In case you haven't heard of RISC-V, it's an openly documented RISC ISA that any individual or commercial hardware vendor can freely implement. Apart from its openness, it boasts a highly modular structure which organizes the specification into a minimal base ISA plus a myriad of optional extensions, enabling highly flexible RISC-V compliant processor designs suited for a wide variety of use cases.

In order to familiarize myself with RISC-V from a programmer's perspective, I've experimented with the following over the past two months (August-September 2022):

As a logical next step to exploring the RISC-V landscape, I've decided to investigate how to take full control of the hardware by writing a RISC-V operating system from scratch.

Current progress (2022-09-10)

Over the past few days, I've managed to implement a rudimentary RISC-V operating system kernel in C that does little more than print stuff to the serial console. Nevertheless, I did my best to structure the project to facilitate development in the mid- to long-term. The project template which I'll build upon going forward is released on GitHub as v0.0.1 of maRVelOS code-named "Meaty Skeleton", so if you're also interested in RISC-V and open hardware as a programmer, feel free to follow along!

I also noticed that the OSDev Wiki is lacking articles and resources on RISC-V at the time of writing, so I took the opportunity to create an account there and contribute my first article, RISC-V Meaty Skeleton with QEMU virt board, in the hopes that it would be useful to the wider OSDev community. As you might have guessed, OSDev stands for "operating systems development" and the OSDev community is where hobbyists in operating systems development gather together to exchange knowledge and ideas.

Resources and acknowledgements

I would like to thank the authors of RISC-V from scratch and The Adventures of OS for their high-quality articles that go into great detail on how RISC-V works. Without their well-written articles, I wouldn't have known how to get started with kernel development on RISC-V. My initial project setup - the minimal C runtime crt0.s in assembly and the linker script riscv64-virt.lds - is based on the former, while my UART driver code is adapted from the latter and I intend to closely follow the latter going forward.

For the cross-compiler toolchain, I referred to the relevant page on the OSDev wiki, and I adapted the instructions from both OSDev and (Beyond) Linux From Scratch for building my cross-GDB targeting 64-bit RISC-V.

My adventure has just begun

maRVelOS can only print stuff to the console at the time of writing. It still can't do a lot of basic stuff expected of a typical operating system:

  • Memory management
  • Process management
  • Handling interrupts
  • Reading and writing files
  • ...

It's probably going to take me a long while before I manage to get anywhere near a borderline usable system, but hopefully I'll stick with it and learn more about the hardware from a programmer's perspective along the way. After all, the main takeaway of OSDev is the learning experience - for an out-of-the-box production grade general-purpose OS kernel, Linux is the answer ;-)
item.author: donaldsebleung

item.title: 为什么流失率变得如此重要？ | Why has the churn rate become so important?
item.description:
巴基詹姆斯

发表于为了提高绩效和提高盈利能力，预测和分析客户流失率至关重要。 对于依赖应用内购买和订阅的企业而言，高客户流失率会对收入产生重大影响。

衡量客户流失率很重要，但预测流失率的能力为营销人员带来了显着的好处。

通过了解哪些客户可能流失以及他们何时流失，营销人员可以在试图留住他们时做出更快的反应。

但是，为了防止客户流失，您应该积极努力防止它，而不是简单地在正确的时间发送赢回报价。

您可以使用Churnfree为您的客户提供可定制的优惠，并获得他们离开的原因的反馈。 这意味着在一天结束时，客户流失是由于定性因素而发生的定量指标。

这对于衡量业务的成功非常重要。


贡献代码库或学习如何托管自己的.

原文:

Bucky James

Posted on Sep 10

To enhance performance and increase profitability, it is essential to predict and analyze the churn rate. When it comes to businesses that rely on in-app purchases and subscriptions, a high customer churn rate can have a significant impact on revenue.

It is important to measure customer churn, but the ability to predict churn rate offers marketers significant benefits.

By understanding which clients are likely to churn and when they do, marketers can react more quickly when attempting to retain them.

To prevent customer churn, however, you should actively work to prevent it rather than simply sending a win-back offer at the right time.

You can use Churnfree to offer your clients customizable offers and get feedback why they are leaving. It means at the end of the day, customer churn is a quantitative indicator that occurs because of qualitative factors.

That is very important to measure the success of the business.

item.title: 通过代币化实现业务数字化 | Business digitization through tokenization
item.description:
由于全球大流行和相关通货膨胀，对银行和投资基金的信心正在下降。 企业家们开始寻找绕过这些类型机构的解决方案。 这种可能性是由代币化提供的，即一种基于区块链技术的数字化形式。

什么是通证化业务数字化？ 如何创建代币，它可以用作收债工具吗？ 我们将从对标记化本身的简短解释开始我们的考虑。

什么是代币化？

由于区块链技术，令牌化成为可能。 区块链是一种去中心化的数据库，是一种用于收集数据的技术。 由于其性质，区块链通常被称为分布式账本技术。 根据特定规则将数据添加到注册表中，并且每个条目都链接到前一个条目。 区块链最显着的特点之一是输入到其中的数据实际上是不可磨灭和不可改变的。

代币化是将价值转化为数字资源的一种形式，是全球最大的区块链技术用例之一。 数字化本身，例如在会计师的工作中，并不是什么新鲜事。 在会计或财务操作、电子文档、电子发票和电子签名中使用计算机程序，或将文档存储在云中是许多公司的日常生活。

什么是令牌？

令牌的概念非常广泛。 在区块链的背景下，代币可以是任何可以以数字方式转移给它的东西。 代币是在区块链上被跟踪或交易的任何东西，尽管在大多数情况下，代币一词是指本地代币（例如比特币或以太坊，在其各自的区块链上）或由智能合约定义的数字资产，它实现了令牌标准。

代币可以是资产的数字表示——数字世界中价值的表示。

我们主要将代币分为可替代代币（包括支付、实用和安全代币）和不可替代代币。

令牌可以是：

  • 某些可转让财产权的数字表示。 然后令牌确认其持有者有权享有的权利。 数字产权可以是数字资产或固定资产，例如房地产；
  • 一份，
  • 分享的权利。 通过代币化实现业务数字化

为什么要对您的业务进行代币化？ 区块链提供的透明度、公开性和可信度支持传统商业模式向数字领域的转移。 存储在区块链上的数据无法被操纵，它是公开可用且可靠的。

观察新技术的发展及其在商业现实中的实施，可以得出结论，现在是普及区块链技术和基于它的代币的时候了。 尽管如此，许多公司在这个问题上提出的问题多于他们得到的答案，这阻碍了发展。 业务代币化基于创建与特定项目密切相关的代币。 项目价值转移到代币的代币化过程可以用作简化和自动化企业流程的工具，并成为其融资的一种方法。

资产代币化

一个有趣的代币化资产类别是房地产。 这个过程越来越受欢迎，因为它的特点是交易速度快、缺乏中介、安全和透明。 该过程基于在区块链上发行代币，然后将它们分配给特定的财产。 重要的是，代币化可能适用于已经存在或正在建设中的房地产。 代币还可以代表对房地产的兴趣。

项目融资：ICO和STO

代币化已成功用于 ICO 和 STO，这是筹集资金的形式。 发行自己的代币意味着更大的影响力和控制力。

在这种情况下，用户的动机是承诺与项目开发相关的代币价格上涨。

这些类型的代币代表了公司在区块链上发行的股票的价值。 拥有基于股票的投资代币使投资者有权获得公司利润并赋予他们投票权。 在大多数司法管辖区，证券型代币保证投资者遵守证券法。

代币化是一种新的、有趣的、繁荣的为投资者和发行人筹集资金的形式。

用 NFT 代币投资艺术品

在购买数字作品（例如图形文件）时，购买者会收到一个 NFT 代币，代表产权。 通证化打破了艺术品市场高门槛的门槛。 由于其独特性，不可交换代币可能代表对艺术品权利的兴趣，这允许投资者投资部分作品。 拍卖行和艺术家开始允许这种形式的有形和无形艺术投资。

代币化业务

如果你想创建自己的代币，一开始你应该认真考虑它们的用途以及它们应该在项目中实现什么功能。 在将您的业务或其部分转移到数字领域的过程中，诸如代币化类型的选择和问题的法律形式等法律问题极为重要。 考虑您是选择不保证买方任何所有权的 ICO，还是选择保护买方的 STO，因为它是资产的数字表示。

决定代币化的企业家应制定单独的 AML 和 KYC 程序。 还需要有一份白皮书，即企业家从技术和法律方面分析产品或服务，并描述项目和未来计划的报告。

有许多方面需要注意，但它们都是为了保护为项目提供资金的投资者。 随着时间的推移，这将受到越来越多的监管，在这种情况下这是一件好事。



原文:

The term "tokenization" has been gaining enormous popularity recently, especially in business environments. As a result of the global pandemic and related inflation, confidence in banks and investment funds is declining. Entrepreneurs are starting to look for solutions to bypass these types of institutions. This possibility is offered by tokenization, i.e. a form of digitization based on blockchain technology.

What is business digitization through tokenization? How to create a token and can it be used as a debt collection tool? We will start our considerations with a short explanation of the tokenization itself.

What is tokenization?

Tokenization is possible thanks to blockchain technology. Blockchain is a decentralized database, a technology used to collect data. Due to its nature, blockchain is often referred to as a distributed ledger technology. Data is added to the registry according to specific rules, and each entry is linked to the previous one. One of the most characteristic features of blockchain is that the data entered into it is practically indelible and unchangeable.

Tokenization is a form of transforming value into digital resources, one of the largest global use cases for blockchain technology. Digitization itself, e.g. in the work of accountants, is nothing new. The use of computer programs in accounting or financial operations, electronic documents, e-invoices, and electronic signatures, or storing documents in a cloud are everyday life for many companies.

What is a token?

The concept of a token is very broad. In the context of blockchain, a token can be anything that can be digitally transferred to it. A token is anything that is tracked or transacted on a blockchain, although in most contexts the term token refers to a native token (e.g. bitcoin or ethereum, on its respective blockchains) or a digital asset defined by a smart contract that implements one of the token standards.

The token can be a digital representation of an asset - a representation of value in the digital world.

We divide tokens primarily into fungible tokens (including payment, utility, and security tokens) and non-fungible tokens.

The token can be:

  • a digital representation of certain transferable property right. Then the token confirms the right that its holder is entitled to. A digital property right can be to a digital asset or a stationary asset, e.g. real estate;
  • A share,
  • A right to share. Business digitization through tokenization

Why tokenize your business? The transparency, disclosure, and credibility provided by the blockchain support the transfer of the traditional business model to the digital sphere. The data stored on the blockchain cannot be manipulated, it is publicly available and reliable.

Observing the development of new technologies and their implementation in business reality, it can be concluded that the time has come to popularize blockchain technology and tokens based on it. Still, many companies ask more questions in this matter than they receive answers, which inhibits development. Business tokenization is based on the creation of tokens closely connected to a specific project. The tokenization process, during which the value of the project is transferred to the token, can be used as a tool to simplify and automate processes in the enterprise and be a method of its financing.

Asset tokenization

An interesting category of tokenized assets is real estate. This process is gaining more and more popularity, as it is characterized by the speed of transactions, lack of intermediaries, security, and transparency. The process is based on issuing tokens on the blockchain and then assigning them to a specific property. Importantly, tokenization may apply to real estate that already exists or is under construction. The token can also represent an interest in real estate.

Financing of the project: ICO and STO

Tokenization is successfully used for ICO and STO, which are forms of raising capital. Issuing own token means greater influence and control.

ICO (Initial Coin Offering), which is the most popular method of raising capital in the form of cryptocurrencies or tokens, is used to finance projects, most often start-ups in to form of crowdfunding. In this case, users are motivated by the promise of the rising price of the token related to the development of the project.

STO (Security Token Offering) - security tokens are backed by company shares or equity. These types of tokens represent the value of shares issued by companies on the blockchain. Owning stock-based investment tokens entitles investors to company profits and gives them the right to vote. Security tokens, in most jurisdictions, guarantee investors compliance with securities laws.

Tokenization is a new, interesting, and prospering form of raising capital for investors and issuers.

Investing in art with NFT tokens

NFT (non-fungible tokens) tokens are very well suited to support investing in the art market, especially the digital one. When buying a digital work, e.g. a graphic file, the buyer receives an NFT token, representing the property right. Tokenization breaks the barrier of a high entry threshold on the art market. Due to their uniqueness, non-exchangeable tokens may represent an interest in the rights to a work of art, which allows investors to invest in part of the work. Auction houses and artists are beginning to allow this form of investment in both tangible and intangible art.

Tokenizing a business

If you want to create your own tokens, at the very beginning you should seriously consider what they are for and what function they should fulfill in the project. Legal issues such as the choice of the type of tokenization and the legal form of the issue are extremely important in the process of transferring your business or its part to the digital sphere. Consider whether you are choosing an ICO that does not guarantee the buyer any proprietary rights or an STO that protects the buyer because it is a digital representation of the asset.

The entrepreneur, deciding on tokenization, should develop individual AML and KYC procedures. It is also necessary to have a whitepaper, i.e. a report in which the entrepreneur analyzes the product or service from the technological and legal side, and describes the project and future plans for it.

There are many aspects that need to be taken care of, but they all serve the protection of the investors that give their money to the project. With time this will be more and more regulated, which in this case is a good thing.
item.author: mrozansk1

item.title: Kotlin Springboot -- 第 9 部分 领域 に中身の比较のドメインロジックを生やして活用する | Kotlin Springboot -- Part 9 Domain に中身の比較のドメインロジックを生やして活用する
item.description:
用例に处理の呼び出しを书く

fun getU10Persons (): Persons { val persons = personsGateway . getAllPersons () val age = Age ( 10 ) return persons . filterByLessThan ( age ) }

网关から呼び出したドライバーの处理をドメインに诘め替えた结果。

このドメインの中のロジックに、さらに 年龄ドメインを渡して実行する。

域に处理を书く

人のドメインに书いて终わりではない。

 data class Persons ( val list : List < Person >) { fun filterByLessThan ( age : Age ): Persons { return Persons ( this . list . filter { it . isLessThan ( age ) }) } }

人は内部で 人（本）一つ一つをマップして 条件に当てはまるものだけを返している。 そのを 人のドメインに书いている。

 data class Person ( val name : Name , val age : Age ) { fun isLessThan ( age : Age ): Boolean { return this . age . isLessThan ( age ) } }

人のドメインでは 人の年龄 に生えている isLessThan の结果を返している

这个で中身の年龄インスタンスにアクセスできる。

 data class Age ( val value : Int ) { fun isLessThan ( other : Age ): Boolean { return this . value < other . value } }

そして 年龄の中に比较关数の本体がある。

それに対して引数の比较対象は other.value にしている。

これ(this)と他(other)の比较。そういう。

これで人ドメインで是LessThan というドメインロジックが机能する。

结果

查询でかえられるともっと面白くて実用的かも知れない

@GetMapping ( "/persons/{age}" ) @ResponseBody fun getPersonsByAge ( @PathVariable ( "age" ) age : Int ): String { val persons = personsUsecase . getPersonsByAge ( Age ( age ))

Rest で PathVar で Int で受け取って Usecase に渡すまえに Age に诘めて

fun getPersonsByAge ( age : Age ): Persons { val persons = personsGateway . getAllPersons () return persons . filterByLessThan ( age ) }

之后は中で呼べば引数からの绞り込みが完成した。使いやすい。

文森特 A. Cicirello - 一旦暂停，kaede_io 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，kaede_io 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，kaede_io 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 kaede_io 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 kaede_io 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 kaede_io 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Usecase に処理の呼び出しを書く 

 fun getU10Persons():Persons { val persons = personsGateway.getAllPersons() val age = Age(10) return persons.filterByLessThan(age) }

Gateway から呼び出したドライバーの処理をドメインに詰め替えた結果。

このドメインの中のロジックに、さらに Age ドメインを渡して実行する。

Domain に処理を書く

Persons のドメインに書いて終わりではない。

data class Persons(val list: List<Person>) { fun filterByLessThan(age: Age): Persons { return Persons(this.list.filter { it.isLessThan(age) }) }
}

Persons は内部で Person (this) 一つ一つをマップして 条件に当てはまるものだけを返している。 その条件を Person のドメインに書いている。

data class Person(val name: Name, val age: Age) { fun isLessThan(age: Age): Boolean { return this.age.isLessThan(age) }
}

Person のドメインでは Person の Age に生えている isLessThan の結果を返している

This で中身の Age インスタンスにアクセスできる。

data class Age(val value: Int) { fun isLessThan(other: Age): Boolean { return this.value < other.value }
}

そして Age の中に比較関数の本体がある。

this.value が Person ドメインに入っている Age の実際の数値。

それに対して引数の比較対象は other.value にしている。

これ(this)と他(other)の比較。そういう意味。

これで Persons ドメインで isLessThan というドメインロジックが機能する。

結果

query でかえられるともっと面白くて実用的かも知れない

 @GetMapping("/persons/{age}") @ResponseBody fun getPersonsByAge(@PathVariable("age") age: Int): String { val persons = personsUsecase.getPersonsByAge(Age(age))

Rest で PathVar で Int で受け取って Usecase に渡すまえに　Age に詰めて

 fun getPersonsByAge(age: Age):Persons { val persons = personsGateway.getAllPersons() return persons.filterByLessThan(age) }

後は中で呼べば引数からの絞り込みが完成した。使いやすい。

Jigar Brahmbhatt - Jul 11

Vincent A. Cicirello - Jul 8

item.title: 在 Java 中优化您的字符串算法 | Optimise your String Algorithms in Java
item.description:
陶西夫·贾马尔

发表于在练习 DSA 字符串问题时，我多次偶然发现的一件事是试图找到替代方案，以使我的算法在某些规模上更快。 因此我探索了不同的方法，在字符串问题中，我必须在一种方法或其他方法之间进行选择，所以我过去常常检查字符串操作的时间和空间复杂性。 我花了几个谷歌搜索来找到一个令人满意的解释，但对于每一个操作我都必须谷歌。

因此，为了减轻您的痛苦并节省您的时间：我列出了字符串上各种操作的时间和空间复杂性，并附有简单的解释。 请注意，这都是针对 Java SE 7 或 v1.7

实例化——新字符串（“a”）

时间复杂度：O(n)

它创建一个 char 数组并填充数组中字符串的每个字符。

串联——“a”+“b”

时间复杂度：O(mn)

每次连接字符串时，都会创建一个新缓冲区并复制内容。

字符串在 Java 中是不可变的。

 Example: Lets say you concatenate "abc + "def" Under the hood, Java is performing these operations: • Construct copy of "abc" with additional length; • Copy "d" to new array • Construct copy of "abcd" • Copy "e" to new array • Construct copy of "abcde" • Copy "f" to new array So O(m) called n times, thus time complexity is O(mn)

空间复杂度：O(mn)

更好的方法：StringBuilder

将 StringBuilder 与 .append() 方法一起使用，它会创建仅按需扩展的内部缓冲区。 它的时间复杂度几乎是 O(m+n)。
时间复杂度：O(1)

因为字符串包含一个计数器变量。

时间复杂度：O(n)

在大多数情况下，它实际上比 O(n) 快，因为它会事先检查一些事情，比如字符串的长度不相等，然后立即返回 false。 它也只线性比较第一个不相等的字符，但在最坏的情况下它的 O(n)

空间复杂度：O(1)

时间复杂度：O(1)

这是因为在Java中，字符串是使用char数组实现的，随机访问是数组的本质。 因此，可以在 O(1) 中随机访问索引处的字符。

时间复杂度：O(n)

如前所述，字符串在 Java 中是不可变的。 因此，要创建长度为 n 的子字符串，需要创建一个新的字符串实例。 在底层，java 实际上是在创建一个长度为 n 的 char 数组，并将元素从原始字符串复制到新字符串。

空间复杂度：O(n)

它需要空间来存储数组的内容。

时间复杂度：O(n)

它应该是 O(1)，因为 string 只不过是一个 char 数组，对吧？ 是的，但是没有。 空间复杂度：O(n)

显然，它需要空间来存储数组的内容。

时间复杂度：O(mn)

令人惊讶的是，java 使用一种天真的方法（循环内循环）来查找另一个字符串。 有几种算法可以像 KMP 一样在 O(n) 中完成相同的工作，但对它们来说也存在开销空间和时间成本。 因此，Sun/Oracle 的工程师必须对各种算法进行了经验测试，并确定朴素方法平均适用于所有类型的场景。

空间复杂度：O(1)

时间复杂度：O(n)

它基本上遍历字符串中的每个字符，并用新字符替换它。 虽然有一些优化

空间复杂度：O(n)

由于字符串是不可变的，因此需要创建新字符串

时间复杂度：哦（很复杂）

无论您的正则表达式字符串是像“red”这样的简单字符串还是复杂的正则表达式模式，这个函数在底层使用 Patterns 类来匹配字符然后替换它。 让我们试着在这里理解时间复杂度的本质。 假设您的正则表达式字符串是 he(ll|ter|r)o

它可以匹配字符串中的 hello、hetero、hero。

正则表达式引擎将遍历字符串，如果遇到 he，它会立即匹配它，然后它会立即尝试匹配 ll，如果找到，它会接下来找到 o，如果没有找到，它会回去尝试匹配 ter，你就明白了。

好吧，这个函数的时间复杂度不在我们的控制范围内，但我们可以优化我们的正则表达式以在匹配方面表现良好。 从前面的例子中，我们可以做 (hello | hetero | hero) 所以它减少了正则表达式引擎的回溯以优化搜索功能并最终替换功能。 如果您想了解有关优化正则表达式模式的更多信息，这是一个很好的资源。
奖励：我找到了一个可视化正则表达式的工具

空间复杂度：O(n)

还记得我们不能在 Java 中改变字符串吗？

如果分隔符是单个字符并且不在“.$|()[{^?*+\”中：

时间复杂度：O(n)

空间复杂度：O(n)

如果分隔符字符串不止一个字符，它会被 Java 编译成正则表达式模式以查找要拆分的索引，如 .replace() 部分所述，它的时间复杂度取决于模式的性质

时间复杂度：哦（很复杂）

空间复杂度：O(n)——因为 y'kno 不可变

时间复杂度：O(n)

空间复杂度：O(n)

直截了当，字符串的每个字符都被逐一检查，新的字符串单独存放

这就是所有的人。 希望本文对您优化字符串函数有所帮助。 我错过了任何基本功能吗？ 让我知道是否有。
谢谢。

向 DEV 的最新成员问好。

阿布舍克·乔杜里—— 阿布舍克·乔杜里—— 阿布舍克·乔杜里—— 一旦暂停，taosif7 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，taosif7 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，taosif7 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 taosif7 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 taosif7 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 taosif7 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Taosif Jamal

Posted on Sep 10

While practicing DSA String problems, one thing I stumbled upon many times was trying to find alternatives to make my algorithm faster at some scales. Thus I explored different approaches, and during String questions I had to choose between one way or other so I used to check time & space complexities of operations on string. It took me few google searches to find a satisfactory explaination, but for each operation I gotta google.

Hence to relieve you from this pain, and to save your time: I am listing Time & Space complexities of various operations on string with straightforward explainations. Note that this is all with respect to Java SE 7 or v1.7

Instantiation — new String(“a”)

Time Complexity: O(n)

It creates a char array and fills each character of string in array.

Concatenation — “a” + ”b”

Time Complexity: O(mn)

Every time you concat a string a new buffer is created and the contents are copied over.

Strings are immutable in Java.

Example: Lets say you concatenate "abc + "def"
Under the hood, Java is performing these operations:
• Construct copy of "abc" with additional length; 
• Copy "d" to new array
• Construct copy of "abcd"
• Copy "e" to new array
• Construct copy of "abcde"
• Copy "f" to new array
So O(m) called n times, thus time complexity is O(mn)

Space Complexity: O(mn)

Better Approach: StringBuilder

Use StringBuilder with .append() method, It creates internal buffer that expands only on demand. Its time complexity is almost O(m+n).
String.concat is another approach, but slower than StringBuilder.

.length()

Time Complexity: O(1)

Because string holds a counter variable.

.equals()

Time Complexity: O(n)

In most cases, its actually faster than O(n), because it checks for some things before hand like length of string is not equal then instantly return false. Also it linearly compares upto first non equal character only, but in the worst case its O(n)

Space Complexity: O(1)

.charAt()

Time Complexity: O(1)

It is because in Java, Strings are implemented using char array, and random access is in array’s nature. Hence character at index can be accessed randomly in O(1).

.substring()

Time Complexity: O(n)

As mentioned before, strings are immutable in Java. Hence for creating a substring of length n, a new string instance needs to be created. Under the hood, java is actually creating a char array of length n and copying elements from original string to new one.

Space Complexity: O(n)

It’ll need space to store contents of array.

.toCharArray()

Time Complexity: O(n)

It should be O(1) since string is nothing but a char array, right? Yes, but no. toCharArray method copies the contents of string to a new char array, hence O(n).

Space Complexity: O(n)

Obviously, it’ll need space to store contents of array.

.contains() and .indexOf()

Time Complexity: O(mn)

.contains() calls .indexOf() method and returns true if index > -1.
It is quite surprising that java uses a naive method (Loop inside loop) for finding a string in another. There are several algorithms out there that do the same job in O(n) like KMP, but for them there’s overhead space and time cost as well. So engineers at Sun/Oracle must have empirically tested various algorithms and decided that naive method works best on average for all kind of scenarios.

Space Complexity: O(1)

.replace(char, char)

Time Complexity: O(n)

It basically goes through each character in string, and replaces it with new character. Although with some optimisations

Space Complexity: O(n)

Since strings are immutable, new string needs to be created

.replace(regex, replacement) and .replaceAll()

Time Complexity: Oh(its complicated)

Whether your regex string is simple string like “red” or its a complex regular expression pattern, this function under the hood uses Patterns class to match the character and then replace it. Lets try to understand the nature of time complexity here. Suppose your regex string is he(ll|ter|r)o

It can match hello, hetero, hero from your string.

The Regex Engine will go through the string, and if it encounters he it’ll intstantly match it, immediately after that it’ll try to match ll if found, it’ll next find o and if o is not found, it’ll go back and try to match ter and you get the point.

Well, Time complexity of this function is not in our control, but we can optimise our Regex to perform well at matching. From previous example, we can do (hello | hetero | hero) So it reduces back tracking by regex engine to optimise search function and ultimately replace function. Here’s a good resource if you want to learn more about optimising regex patterns.
Bonus: I found a tool to Visualise Regex

Space Complexity: O(n)

Remember we can’t mutate a string in Java?

.split(separator)

If Separator is single character and not in “.$|()[{^?*+\”:

Time Complexity: O(n)

Space Complexity: O(n)

If Separator string is more than one character, it is compiled into a Regex Pattern by Java for finding index to split, and as discussed in .replace() section, It’s time complexity depends on nature of pattern

Time Complexity: Oh(its complicated)

Space Complexity: O(n)— Because y’kno immutable

.toLowerCase() and .toUpperCase()

Time Complexity: O(n)

Space Complexity: O(n)

Straightforward, each character of string is checked one by one, and new string is stored separately

That’s all Folks. I hope this article will be helpful to you to optimise your string functions. Did I miss any essential function? let me know if any.
Thanks.

Say hello to the newest members of DEV.

Abhishek Chaudhary - Jun 18

Abhishek Chaudhary - Jun 18

Abhishek Chaudhary - Jun 18

item.title: 什么是流失率？ | What is Churn Rate?
item.description:

如果您在任何给定时间有 5000 名客户，并且一个月内有 300 名客户离开，那么您该月的流失率为 6%。

item.title: 确保 Internet 上信息安全的 11 条提示 | 11 Tips for Keeping Information Safe on the Internet
item.description:
互联网带来了许多充满乐趣的活动和获取信息的途径，这是我们十年前做梦也想不到的。 虽然今天的互联网是一个很好的资源，但必须记住它也可能是危险的，特别是如果你有孩子在你不看的时候“研究”。

在家、在学校或在工作中不乏风险。 从不适当的内容到恶意网站等等，都存在许多风险。 本博客将介绍一些在线安全提示，以帮助您在互联网上和互联网外保护您的个人详细信息、私人照片和视频的安全。

保持个人数据的私密性和有限性
潜在雇主或消费者不需要您的个人或财务信息。 他们必须了解您的技能和专业经验以及如何与您联系。 你不会把你的个人信息透露给陌生人，那为什么还要在网上把它透露给无数无名的人呢？

制作强大而安全的密码
创建密码时，请考虑恶意行为者可能容易破译的过去短语或数字，例如您的出生日期或家人的姓名。 定期更改大小写字母、数字和字符。

为了互联网安全，生成不同且强密码而不是在不同站点上使用相同的密码也是一个好主意 - 密码库或密码管理器应用程序可以帮助维护此记录。

在线浏览时要小心
你不会冒险进入现实生活中的危险领域，所以不要上网访问不安全的网站。 身份窃贼经常利用耸人听闻的信息作为诱饵。 他们意识到网民偶尔会被可疑内容所吸引和好奇，并且在搜索时可能会放松警惕。

互联网的底层充满了未知的危险——一次轻率的点击可能会泄露个人信息和敏感数据，或者让您的小工具被病毒污染。 如果您抵制这种欲望，您甚至不会为在线掠夺者提供访问您敏感信息的机会。

两步验证是必须的
大型知名组织，如 PayPal、Facebook、Google 和其他公司使用两步验证，要求用户使用发送到手机的代码登录。

其他组织可能需要您的手机号码或备用电子邮件帐户，以便在他们检测到任何可疑活动或有人试图从其他设备访问该帐户时，您将收到要求额外验证的消息。

警惕你分享的内容
因为删除原件并不能真正删除其他人生成的任何副本，所以您在网上发布的任何评论或图片都可能无限期地保留在网上。

您不能“收回”您可能会后悔发布的评论或删除您在聚会上点击的尴尬自拍。 为了确保上网安全，不要在互联网上发布任何您不希望您的母亲或未来雇主看到的内容。

使用免费 Wi-Fi 时要谨慎
从来没有人说过一点点在线购物是不好的，或者有人说过吗？ 您可能用于在线购物的任何免费公共 Wi-Fi 网络都没有足够的互联网安全保护； 同一互联网连接上的其他人可以很容易地看到您在做什么。

在取出信用卡之前，请确保您在家，连接到安全、受密码保护的虚拟专用网络，并安装了防病毒软件。

检查您的银行和债权人的保护措施
没有关于在线活动的通用互联网安全和保护之类的东西。 如果您在网上购物或在互联网上开展任何业务，请确保您的银行或信贷机构有专门的程序，以在发生在线诈骗和身份盗窃时保护您。 如果您的帐户被黑客入侵和利用，您不想自我保险。

此外，永远不要让您的浏览器或网页记住您的银行帐户信息，因为这肯定会导致麻烦。

在线与人会面时要小心
您在社交媒体平台上联系或在网上认识的人并不总是他们所说的那样。 它们甚至可能不是真实的。 伪造的在线帐户是黑客依偎天真的互联网用户并清空他们的在线钱包的便捷方法。

为防止数据泄露，请在您的互联网社区生活中保持与离线社交生活相同的谨慎和常识水平。

留意连接和附件
在线犯罪分子和黑客非常狡猾。 他们的网络钓鱼诈骗通常伪装成来自机构、电力公司或其他企业的信息。 特定特征（例如拼写错误或不寻常的电子邮件帐户）可能表明邮件来自恶意软件。

驱动器擦除和恢复出厂设置
通常，只是从您的计算机或移动设备中“删除”任何内容或清除您的浏览历史记录并不会导致这些信息从小工具中永久删除。 当您转售或丢弃旧的台式计算机或移动设备时，请确保彻底清洁驱动器或云存储并且系统已恢复出厂设置。

寻求信息并检查细则
客户应阅读并检查网页的安全措施，以了解网站/平台将如何以及为何使用他们的个人信息。

因此，企业应该制定有据可查的隐私规则和标准，并能够正确解决出现的任何问题。

底线
随着互联网已成为我们日常生活的一部分，它的不安全感也随之而来。 安全地上网和遵循健康的上网习惯并不仅仅意味着要小心您点击的内容。 这意味着了解如何保护您的身份并确保您的在线个人信息不会落入坏人之手。

在签字之前，要记住的最重要的事情是，虽然互联网是一种极好的资源，但它不能替代现实生活中的联系。 因此，通过聪明、使用上面提到的互联网安全提示以及知道如何在需要时获得帮助，确保在线和离线保持安全。



原文:

The internet brings with it a host of fun-filled activities and access to information that we could never have dreamt of just a decade ago. While the internet today is a wonderful resource, it is essential to remember that it can also be hazardous, especially if you have children who are "researching" while you are not watching.

There is no shortage of risks at home, at school, or at work. From inappropriate content to malicious sites and more, there are numerous risks. This blog will look at a few online safety tips to help you keep your personal details, private photos, and videos safe on the internet and off the internet, too.

Keep Personal Data Private and Limited
Potential employers or consumers do not require your personal or financial information. They must be aware of your skills and professional experience and how to contact you. You wouldn't dish out your personal details to strangers, so why give it out to countless, nameless people online?

Make Strong and Secure Passwords
When creating passwords, think of past phrases or figures that a malicious actor might easily decipher, such as your birthdate or your family members' names. Change the uppercase and lowercase letters, digits, and characters regularly.

For internet safety, it's also a good idea to generate distinct and strong passwords rather than using the same password on different sites - a password vault or password manager application can help maintain a record of this.

Be Cautious When Browsing Online
You wouldn't venture into a risky area in real life, so don't go online and visit unsecured websites. Identity thieves often utilize lurid information as bait. They realize netizens are occasionally attracted and curious about questionable content and may possibly lay their guard down when searching for it.

The underbelly of the internet is fraught with unknown dangers - a single thoughtless click could potentially reveal personal information and sensitive data or contaminate your gadget with viruses. You don't even offer the online predators a chance to gain access to your sensitive information if you resist the desire.

Two-Step Authentication is a Must
Large, reputable organizations such as PayPal, Facebook, Google, and others utilize two-step verification, requiring users to sign in using a code texted to their cellphones.

Other organizations may want your cellphone number or an alternate email account so that if they detect any suspicious activity or someone tries to access the account from other devices, you will receive a message requesting extra verification.

Be Wary of What You Share
The internet server doesn't have a true delete feature. Because eliminating the original doesn't really erase any copies generated by others, any comment or picture you post online may remain online indefinitely.

You can't "claim back" a comment you might regret posting or delete an awkward selfie you clicked at a party. To stay safe online, post nothing on the internet that you'd never want your mother or a future employer to see.

Use Prudence When Using Free Wi-Fi
No one ever said that a tad bit of online shopping was bad, or did someone? Any free public Wi-Fi network that you may use for making online purchases will have insufficient internet safety protections in place; other people on the same internet connection could readily see what you're doing.

Before pulling out your credit card, be sure you're at home, connected to a safe, password-protected virtual private network, and have antivirus software installed.

Examine Your Bank's and Creditor's Protection Measures
There is no such thing as universal internet safety and protection regarding online activities. If you shop online or do any business on the internet, ensure your bank or credit institutions have procedures designed to safeguard you in the event of online scams and identity theft. If your account has been hacked and exploited, you don't want to self-insure.

Furthermore, never enable your browser or web pages to remember your bank account information, for this is a sure-fire way to end up in trouble.

Take Caution When Meeting People Online
Individuals you connect with on social media platforms or meet online aren't always who they say they are. It's possible that they're not even real. Forged online accounts are a convenient method for hackers to snuggle up to naive internet users and empty their online wallets.

To prevent a data breach, maintain the same level of caution and common sense in your internet community life as in your offline social life.

Keep an Eye Out for Connections and Attachments
Online criminals and hackers are quite crafty. Their phishing scams are usually disguised as messages from an institution, power company, or other business. Specific characteristics, such as misspellings or an unusual email account, can indicate that the message is from malicious software.

Drive Wiping and Factory Reset
Frequently, just "deleting" anything from your computer or mobile device or clearing your browsing history does not result in the information being permanently removed from the gadget. When you resell or discard your old desktop computers or mobile devices, ensure the drives or cloud storage are thoroughly cleaned and the system is the factory reset.

Seek Information and Examine the Fine Print
Web browsers must understand that safeguarding personal information is a shared duty among themselves and their organizations. Customers should read and examine the security measures of the web pages to understand how and why the site/platform will utilize their personal information.

As a result, businesses should have well-documented privacy rules and standards in place and the ability to resolve any issues that arise correctly.

Bottom Line
As the internet has become a part of our daily lives, so have its insecurities. Safely Surfing the web and following healthy internet habits doesn't just mean being careful what you click. It means understanding how to protect your identity and ensure that your personal information online doesn't fall into the wrong hands.

Before signing off, the most important thing to remember is that, while the internet is a fantastic resource, it is not a substitute for real-life connections. So make sure to stay safe online and offline by being smart, using the internet safety tips mentioned above, and knowing how to get help if you need it.
